Defendants Jpmorgan Chase Bank And California Reconveyance Company'S Motion To Dismiss For Plaintiffs' Failure To Amend Complaint Following Sustaining Of Demurrer
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Atty. Gene V. halavanau of HerefordKerley LLP (510-379-5801) appearing for plaintiff Gary Verkholaz opposing party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Jpmorgan Chase Bank And California Reconveyance Company'S Motion To Dismiss For Plaintiffs' Failure To Amend Complaint Following Sustaining Of Demurrer. Granted Motion to Dismiss in part. Second and Fourth causes of action dismissed with prejudice. Plaintiff shall file amended complaint within 10 days alleging the one remaining cause of action. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Ronald E Quidachay, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (501/REQ)

Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss For Pltfs Failure To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Dismiss For Plaintiff's Failure To Amend Complaint is transferred to be heard in Department 501 on the same date at the same time. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Continued To Jan-08-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by J. Edward Kerley, Esq. for Plaintiff; no appearance by Defendant's counsel. Defense Counsel is sanctioned $350.00 for failure to appaer. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to JAN-08-2013 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Defendants Jp Morgan Chase Bank And California Reconveyance Company'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs' Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances - Court adopted its tentative Ruling. Defendants Jp Morgan Chase Bank And California Reconveyance Company'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs' Complaint. Overrule, as to 1st cause of action - allegations presumed true not contradicted by Judicially noticed documents; Gomes v. Countrywide inapposite. Sustain with leave to amend as to 2nd and 4th causes of action. Sustain without leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action. Injunctive relief rememdy not cause of action. Moving party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, not reported. (501/REQ)
